notes: Verified via WebSearch 2026-07-11 (Wikipedia, TAP official news agency, Inkyfada): April 9, 1938 — French colonial forces killed 22 and wounded ~150 Tunisian demonstrators demanding an elected parliament; two marches (led by Ali Belhouane from Halfaouine, Mongi Slim from Rahbet El Ghanem) converged near the French Résidence Générale, 7,000-10,000 participants.
Verified women's first documented participation in a major Tunisian national protest movement during these events.
Confirmed official paid public holiday status since April 1957 (post-independence government decision), still in effect.
Historical colonial-violence content handled with respectful, factual national register, no contemporary political framing.
Single-country event (_countryCode: tn), aliasSlugs: [] to avoid base_country_leakage.
